factors.name: Iran Deal De-escalation Relief
factors.summary: Trump announced framework agreement with Iran, canceling planned military strikes. Markets rallied sharply: Nikkei +4%, Korean Kospi +8%, oil fell to $85/bbl (lowest since mid-April). This reduces geopolitical risk premium that has weighed on growth equities.

factors.name: Risk-On Sentiment & Equity Flows
factors.summary: NAS100 +2.12% in 24h as de-escalation lifts risk appetite. Nasdaq-listed semiconductors and tech rallied globally. However, move is already in progress post-Thursday de-escalation announcement.

factors.name: Hot US PPI Inflation Data
factors.summary: US Producer Price Index came in hotter than expected in May per FXStreet/Reuters reporting. This undermines the dovish narrative and lifts real yields, pressuring long-duration growth stocks and multiples.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY trading near 99.80 (+gains) driven by hot PPI and Middle East uncertainty. Stronger USD headwinds for NAS100 mega-cap international revenue (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL have 40-50% revenue overseas).

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations & Real Yields
factors.summary: No explicit FOMC news in window, but hot PPI + RBA hold expectations (per Reuters poll) suggest global central banks pausing/on hold. This limits duration relief for equities; real yields remain sticky despite de-escalation rally.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Window Closed / No Guidance
factors.summary: No recent earnings catalysts from AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA in this news window. Adobe CFO exit raises strategy uncertainty but is idiosyncratic. Awaiting next earnings season for forward capex/AI demand commentary.

factors.name: Nasdaq 100 Index Changes (Rocket Lab, SpaceX IPO)
factors.summary: Rocket Lab and 4 AI stocks added to NAS100; SpaceX IPO at $1.8T valuation imminent but low retail allocation (20%). These are rebalancing/composition shifts, not demand drivers for broad index.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tail Risk Still Unresolved
factors.summary: Iran pushback on deal scope, IRGC vows 'regret-inducing' response, tanker blocked from Hormuz, drones intercepted. Trump's 39th 'deal' announcement lacks formal finalization—market pricing optimism ahead of confirmed agreement carries reversal risk.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation (BTC Risk-Off)
factors.summary: Bitcoin under pressure, $1.9B exiting spot ETFs, BTC failing as hedge per Cointelegraph. This suggests underlying risk-off beneath surface enthusiasm, potential warning sign for duration-heavy NQ assets.

factors.name: Volatility Regime (VXN / Options)
factors.summary: No explicit VIX/VXN levels cited, but dealer gamma and positioning data absent from news window. Elevated geopolitical vol + earnings vacuum typical pre-event setup; chop likely into next data/earnings catalyst.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 rallied +2.12% in 24h on Trump's Iran de-escalation narrative, which lifted risk sentiment globally (Nikkei +4%, Kospi +8%) and knocked oil to $85/bbl. However, the move is already in PHASE 2—the relief trade has largely priced in over Thursday-Friday. Underlying headwinds remain: hot US PPI inflation data surprises higher, the USD strengthens to 99.80+ (headwind for tech's international revenues), and real yields stay sticky despite the equity rally. Critically, Iran deal remains unconfirmed with Tehran disputing scope and blocking Hormuz tankers—this is Trump's 39th 'deal' claim, and markets may revert if negotiations stall.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI print (timing TBD—if expected soon, could confirm hot inflation trend and cap upside)
Fed communications / FOMC (no imminent meeting stated; but sticky PPI will dominate Fed narrative)
RBA June 16 meeting (expected hold per Reuters poll; minor AUD/NZD impact but gauges global hawkish bias)
SpaceX IPO trading debut (composition shift for NQ but limited broad demand signal)
Iran deal finalization or collapse (geopolitical tail risk; would reverse current relief trade if talks break)
Mega-cap tech earnings restart (NVDA, META, others TBD; forward guidance on cloud capex / AI demand critical)
Treasury auction (30Y priced at 5.020% Friday; watch for yield re-rating if demand soft)
screening.risk_warning: Move is already 2%+ in 24h (PHASE 2). If Iran negotiations collapse or new military incident occurs, the relief rally reverses sharply. Conversely, if CPI data surprises hot again, real yields re-steepen and duration-heavy Nasdaq growth multiples compress—particularly vulnerable: NVDA, TSLA, AAPL. Late-cycle entry here is risky without a retest or fresh catalyst. Strong dollar / weak international revenues also cap upside if USD momentum sustains.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended post-Thursday catalyst; wait for pullback to 29200–29350 support zone before entering long, or take profits on strength near 29500–29600 resistance. If Iran headlines turn negative, expect gap-down retest of 28800–29000. Do not chase current rally in PHASE 2 without fresh bullish catalyst (e.g., softer-than-expected inflation data or Fed dovish pivot signal).

factors.name: Iran-US Deal Optimism / Risk-Off Reversal
factors.summary: Trump announced framework agreement with Iran; Strait of Hormuz reopening expected within 30 days, sanctions relief phased. Equities rallied strongly on de-escalation; S&P 500 posted largest gain in 2 months per Deutsche Bank commentary. Oil fell below $90/bbl, relieving inflation pressure on growth multiples.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: Brent crude tumbled below $90/bbl on deal hopes (vs $150+ risk scenario if Hormuz remained closed). Lower energy costs reduce stagflation risk, support margin expansion for tech, and reduce Fed hike pressure. Energy shock fears abate.

factors.name: Fed Rate-Hike Expectations Reset Lower
factors.summary: Deal narrative and falling oil prices have repriced Fed hiking cycle lower. Real yields likely declining; long-duration Nasdaq-100 names (high growth, low cash flow today) benefit from lower WACC and multiple expansion.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield (Inverse Duration Effect)
factors.summary: Oil collapse and deal de-escalation typically push nominal yields down; European bonds rallied per Bloomberg. Lower 10Y yield expands growth stock valuations (Nasdaq inverse correlate). No fresh yield data in this window, but tone is clearly dovish.

factors.name: Price Action Already In Motion (+2.12% in 24h)
factors.summary: NAS100 up 2.12% in 24h and +1.42% in 4h — move is in PHASE_2 (underway for several hours). Rally has traction but extends risk of near-term pullback or consolidation before fresh breakout.

factors.name: US Inflation Data (PPI Hot)
factors.summary: US PPI described as 'hot' per FXStreet; this initially BEARISH for equities and supportive of USD strength. However, market is choosing to look through it via oil relief narrative. Conflicting signal that could re-emerge if energy prices stabilize.

factors.name: European Inflation Persistence
factors.summary: France CPI +2.4% YoY (up from +2.2%), Spain core CPI +3.0% (up from +2.8%), Germany core CPI +2.5% (up from +2.3%). Broadening wage/services pressure. ECB stayed hawkish; Makhlouf said ECB 'needs to act quickly.' Higher European rates could drag USD/growth sentiment longer-term.

factors.name: ECB Hawkish Bias / Rate Uncertainty
factors.summary: ECB hiked 25bps yesterday, signalled readiness for July hike if needed. Lagarde avoided forward guidance. This tightening cycle could eventually slow global growth, though near-term oil relief offsets. Commerzbank notes 'complex rate path' ahead for EUR.

factors.name: SpaceX Mega-IPO / AI Mania Spillover
factors.summary: SpaceX $75B IPO indicated +35% higher in shadow markets; described as 'blockbuster.' Elon Musk stock pop, AI/space tech narrative strength. Nasdaq-100 contains heavy TSLA weighting; positive Musk sentiment can drive single-name momentum and sector enthusiasm.

factors.name: Dollar Direction (USD Index +0.30% overnight)
factors.summary: USD Index near 99.80, holding gains despite oil collapse and rate-cut optimism. Divergent signal: PPI hot, but risk-off from Iran means some USD safe-haven bid still present. Neutral for now; watch if USD weakens significantly (would be bullish for NQ growth names' international revenue).

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings / Guidance
factors.summary: No earnings surprises in this 24h window. SpaceX IPO and Kioxia (memory chip) replacement of Toyota as Japan's most valuable firm shows AI/chip momentum, but lacks specific mega-cap (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL) earnings catalysts. Watchlist for next 5-7 days.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain
factors.summary: Kioxia becomes Japan's most valuable firm on AI boom; Avalanche Treasury AVAX on Nasdaq at 5-year low (unrelated to Nasdaq-100). No TSMC, ASML, Samsung earnings or guidance in this window. Neutral pending next earnings cycle.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq-100 rallied sharply on Iran-US deal optimism and oil collapse, gaining +2.12% in 24h as investors repriced Fed hiking odds lower and growth multiple expansion resumed. The de-escalation narrative (Strait of Hormuz reopening, sanctions relief) eliminated stagflation tail risk, allowing tech longs to re-engage. However, the move is now 2–3 hours into Phase 2; early buyers are nursing profits, and late entrants risk near-term pullback or consolidation. Counterwinds include hot US PPI, sticky European core inflation, and ECB hawkish bias, which could re-assert if oil stabilizes or if deal talks falter (IRGC rhetoric remains combative per Fox News report).
catalysts_ahead: Iran-US deal finalization (ongoing negotiations; could break down, re-escalate tensions)
Oil price moves (watch $85–$95 range; any spike back toward $100+ reverses relief narrative)
ECB July rate decision guidance (Nagel, Makhlouf keep hawkish tone alive)
US CPI/PCE next week (inflation persistence would challenge dovish repricing)
FOMC announcement (mid-month; if deal holds, expect rate-cut pricing to accelerate)
Mega-cap tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL in coming 2–4 weeks)
SpaceX trading opens (TSLA sentiment could drive Musk-linked volatility)
screening.risk_warning: Deal collapse or fresh Iran-US military escalation would reverse oil relief, re-ignite stagflation fears, and trigger 2–3% selloff in NAS100. Similarly, if PPI/CPI data next week surprises hot and ECB/Fed maintain hawkish tone, growth multiple compression could undercut the current rally. Move is already +2% in 24h; pullback to 29,200–29,300 would be healthy re-entry zone; chasing here risks late-entry drawdown into weekend or early next week if deal sentiment wavers.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended in Phase 2; wait for pullback to 29,250–29,350 or consolidation above 29,400 before fresh longs. Short pullback play: enter on retest of 29,200 with tight 50-pip stop, targeting 29,100–29,000 if sentiment shifts. Avoid chasing into European close or Friday afternoon (low liquidity, whipsaw risk).

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already extended +2.12% in 24h on Iran deal optimism; wait for pullback toward 29,200-29,100 support or clarity on deal finalization before fresh longs.

factors.name: US-Iran Deal Optimism
factors.summary: Trump announced framework agreement to reopen Strait of Hormuz, lift sanctions; oil prices fell below $90/bbl (Brent), reducing inflation fears and supporting risk appetite. However, Iran's Mehr agency reports MoU 'not yet finalized' and IRGC vows 'regret-inducing response,' signaling significant execution risk.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: Brent crude fell below $90 on deal hopes after Trump canceled strikes; lower energy costs reduce inflation pressures and input costs for corporates, positive for growth multiples. Societe Generale notes sustained downtrend below 50-DMA.

factors.name: Hot US PPI Inflation
factors.summary: US Producer Price Index came in hot (specific figures not disclosed in news but referenced multiple times), lifting USD and reinforcing hawkish Fed expectations. This pressures long-duration tech valuations and reduces room for rate cuts.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Deutsche Bank notes 'reduced Fed hike expectations' helped yesterday's rally, but hot PPI data counters this. ECB hiked 25bps and signaled possible July hike if oil rises, showing global central banks still in tightening mode amid energy-driven inflation.

factors.name: Treasury Yields / Real Yields
factors.summary: Eurozone bonds rose on Iran deal hopes; lower yields would support tech multiples, but hot US PPI likely pressures US real yields higher. No specific 10-year Treasury data provided, creating uncertainty for duration-sensitive NDX names.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: DXY trading around 99.80, holding gains after hot PPI data per news; stronger dollar pressures NDX constituents with heavy international revenue exposure (60%+ for mega-caps). EUR/USD at 1.1565, JPY above 160.00 intervention levels.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Event Risk
factors.summary: SpaceX $75bn IPO (largest ever) pricing today; shadow markets indicate 35%+ pop. While not directly NDX-related initially, massive tech IPO can draw liquidity and shift sentiment. Rocket Lab joining NDX per MarketWatch, signaling index rebalancing flow risk.

factors.name: Equity Risk Appetite
factors.summary: S&P 500 posted largest gain in two months yesterday; European stocks rallied on Iran deal hopes. However, Nasdaq 100 futures down -0.29% to 29,380 at European open, suggesting profit-taking after extended move.

factors.name: Geopolitical Execution Risk
factors.summary: Despite Trump's deal announcement, Fox News reports US forces shot down Iranian drones targeting Hormuz shipping; IRGC vows continued strikes. 60-day ceasefire is MoU only, not finalized, with 'long negotiations to come.' High risk of reversal if deal collapses.

factors.name: China-Japan Tech Dynamics
factors.summary: Kioxia (memory chipmaker) became Japan's most valuable firm on AI boom, displacing Toyota; signals continued AI infrastructure demand. Banks curbing hedge fund leverage on SK Hynix/Samsung after rally suggests positioning stretched in semis.

factors.name: Tech Sector Specific News
factors.summary: Stifel cuts Adobe rating on 'growth strategy shift' per headlines; no mega-cap earnings or major AI capex announcements in this news window. Lack of fresh positive catalysts from NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN front.

factors.name: Extended Price Action
factors.summary: NAS100 already up +2.12% in 24h and +1.42% in 4h; move well underway. Futures down -0.29% at European session suggests early profit-taking and diminished follow-through momentum.

confirmation.narrative: NAS100 rallied sharply (+2.12% in 24h) on US-Iran framework deal hopes and falling oil prices, but the move is now extended and facing headwinds. Hot US PPI data reinforces hawkish Fed expectations, pressuring growth multiples, while stronger USD (99.80) weighs on international revenue. Critical execution risk remains: Iran's MoU is unfinalized, IRGC promises retaliation, and US forces are still downing Iranian drones near Hormuz. Futures turning negative (-0.29%) at European open signals waning momentum. Phase 3 setup: rally already priced, not a fresh entry point.
catalysts_ahead: Iran deal finalization or collapse (next 24-72h critical window)
Weekend geopolitical developments in Strait of Hormuz
Bank of Japan rate decision (widely expected hike to 31-year high, could pressure risk assets)
US CPI data (next major inflation print)
SpaceX IPO trading debut and liquidity impact
NDX rebalancing flows (Rocket Lab and four AI stocks joining per MarketWatch)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Iran deal collapses or Hormuz attacks resume, spiking oil back above $100 and reigniting stagflation fears; (2) follow-through US inflation data (CPI) comes in hot, forcing Fed to maintain/accelerate tightening; (3) major mega-cap earnings disappointment or AI capex guidance cuts. Current neutral stance reflects extended positioning, unfinalized geopolitical catalyst, and conflicting macro signals (lower oil vs. hot PPI/strong USD).

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=BUY vs Sonnet=NEUTRAL — silent log

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Hopes
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports US-Iran MoU could be signed Sunday in Geneva; Trump called off strikes and announced deal framework. Oil falling (WTI $82.90), reducing inflation hedge risk and supporting risk-on sentiment across equities.

factors.name: SpaceX $75B IPO Historic Debut
factors.summary: Largest IPO on record launching on Nasdaq June 12; shadow trading suggests 35% first-day pop. Mega-cap listing confidence, investment banking fees boost, retail risk-on appetite. Direct boost to Nasdaq volume/flow.

factors.name: Real Yields / Fed Policy Stance
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting June 17 expected to deliver neutral stance; earlier rate cut projections likely removed from dot plot. No immediate hawkish surprise, but uncertainty remains—caps upside confidence.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD retracing gains on Iran deal peace hopes; DXY under pressure as safe-haven demand eases. Weaker dollar supports US multinational earnings (NQ heavy foreign revenue) and reduces hedging costs.

factors.name: Tech Mega-Cap Earnings Cycle
factors.summary: Adobe hit with multiple downgrades post-ARR concerns and CFO departure (negative single-name signal). No major earnings from AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA imminent in next 24-48h; macro backdrop overriding individual names.

factors.name: Hyperscaler Capex / AI Demand
factors.summary: Sharon AI surges 25% on Nvidia compute deal; general AI/semiconductor strength persists but no major new capex guidance in recent news. Sector sentiment positive but no fresh catalyst.

factors.name: Institutional Cash & Valuations
factors.summary: BNY notes declining institutional cash balances and elevated valuations amid record IPO issuance. Potential liquidity tightening and crowded momentum trades at risk of rotation/pullback.

factors.name: Options Positioning & Volatility
factors.summary: Space-stock options volumes booming ahead of SpaceX debut; broader VXN not flagged as extreme. Gamma/delta dynamics fluid but no clear skew signal vs. historical positioning.

factors.name: CPI / Inflation Data
factors.summary: India CPI came in 3.93% YoY (May), lower than 4% estimate; US Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index expected weak per FXStreet. No fresh US CPI surprise in this window—next big release will be PCE.

factors.name: Momentum & Price Action
factors.summary: NAS100 +1.42% (4h), +2.12% (24h). Already rallied meaningfully post-Iran news and SpaceX hype—move is in progress and partially priced. Further upside requires sustained momentum or new catalyst.

key_levels.support: 29,100–29,200 (yesterday's close area, retest zone if pullback triggered)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq is in a sustained risk-on rally driven by two major catalysts: the near-certainty of a US-Iran peace deal (reducing geopolitical premium in oil/inflation expectations) and SpaceX's blockbuster $75B IPO debut, which is generating institutional and retail demand. Dollar weakness and lower real-yield expectations support long-duration growth names. However, the move has already occurred (+2.1% in 24h), institutional cash is declining per BNY, valuations remain elevated, and the Fed's Kevin Warsh will hold his first meeting June 17 amid ongoing rate-cut uncertainty. This is Phase 2 momentum—not a fresh entry, but continuation territory.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX Nasdaq debut / first-day trading (happening now, June 12)
US-Iran MoU signing (expected by Sunday June 15 per Bloomberg)
Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index preliminary estimate (June 12, later today)
Fed FOMC Meeting & Kevin Warsh first presser (June 17–18)
CPI / PCE inflation data (dates TBD, likely late June)
SK Hynix Nasdaq listing announcement (semiconductor supply-chain confidence)
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran deal announcement is delayed or weaker-than-expected (geopolitical premium re-inflates oil), or if SpaceX IPO pops but then crashes (valuation shock, retail lock-in selling), momentum breaks and NAS100 could retrace to 29,100–29,200 or below. Additionally, any hawkish surprise from Warsh or a hot PCE print would compress growth multiples and trigger a sharp pullback in long-duration tech. Declining institutional cash and frothy retail participation in IPOs elevate crash risk.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended into Phase 2; avoid chasing rallies above 29,600. Wait for pullback to 29,200–29,350 before considering fresh BUY, or hold existing longs with tight stops at 29,000. Use Warsh FOMC (June 17) as a re-anchor point—if he surprises hawkish, consider covering.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event (largest IPO ever)
factors.summary: SpaceX opened at ~$150-172 vs $135 IPO, massive retail FOMO and headline gravity. However, S&P 500 excluded it, and rival space stocks dumped in sympathy. Capital demand is broad-based (per Citi Kaiser), which pressures equities but concentrates liquidity into SPCX rather than flowing into NQ names.

factors.name: Consumer sentiment beat
factors.summary: UoM Consumer Sentiment June flash: 48.9 vs 46.0 expected (per Bloomberg, 14:06). Current conditions 48.4 vs 46.2 exp. Inflation expectations fell (1-yr: 4.6% vs 4.8%, 5-yr: 3.4% vs 3.9%). Modest relief on growth fears.

factors.name: Iran peace deal uncertainty (volatile on/off narrative)
factors.summary: Deal momentum faded sharply post-Trump statement (13:47) denying terms, calling Iran 'dishonorable.' Oil rebounded off lows; risk-off sentiment returned after brief risk-on surge. Geopolitical tail risk re-engaged; no stable consensus on deal outcome.

factors.name: Semiconductor strength but narrowly distributed
factors.summary: AMD +5.39%, NVDA +0.54%, TXN +1.14% (per Investinglive 14:46). AI capex narrative intact. However, concentrated in single names; broad-based NQ lift remains questionable given overall index chop.

factors.name: Adobe CFO departure + leadership instability
factors.summary: CFO Dan Durn departing June 15, follows CEO resignation earlier in year (per Bloomberg 14:56). Software stocks tumbled in sympathy. Leadership vacuum in mega-cap cloud/AI names adds execution risk.

factors.name: Tech earnings calendar (near-term quiet, but FOMC risk)
factors.summary: No major FAANG earnings flagged in next 72h. However, market structure shows broad capital demand (IPOs, secondaries, bonds), which typically compresses equity risk premiums. Wait for FOMC calendar visibility.

factors.name: Dollar holding firm despite Iran optimism
factors.summary: DXY still supported despite soft-deal narrative (per ING 13:27). Energy relief incomplete; inflation risk remains if oil flow through Strait of Hormuz doesn't materialize. No clear trend in USD yet.

factors.name: Real yields and 10-year Treasury (not explicitly mentioned)
factors.summary: No yield data in news window. Prior bias history shows UoM sentiment beat could ease Fed pause pressure, but without 10-year or TIPS move confirmation, duration impact unclear.

factors.name: Options positioning: call buying in space/tech names
factors.summary: Piper Sandler noted ton of short-dated call buying as FOMO proxy (per 15:18). When retail piles into call flow this aggressively into an already-soared name (SpaceX +17-20%), it is a classic late-stage warning, not early conviction.

factors.name: Phase of NQ move (prior bias history)
factors.summary: Prior reads (June 12): BUY6-7/10 but NO CONFIRMATION triggered (Haiku). Today's news (consumer beat + SpaceX IPO) are incremental, not triggering reversal. Move remains contested; no fresh momentum confirmed.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between conflicting narratives: consumer sentiment beat and semiconductor strength (AMD, NVDA) provide soft BULLISH underpinning, but the SpaceX mega-IPO is sucking retail capital flow into a single name (SPCX now $2T+ valuation), triggering broad-based tech selloff including software (Adobe CFO exit). Iran peace deal momentum collapsed mid-session as Trump denied terms, re-engaging geopolitical risk and deflating the brief risk-on bounce. Options gamma and dealer flows show late-stage FOMO into call spreads, not conviction. Broad capital demand (IPOs, secondaries) is straining equity liquidity.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision + Powell presser (dates not in news, but typically high-impact within 7 days)
CPI/Core CPI print (next major inflation gauge)
Earnings from MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL if this week
Iran peace deal finalization or collapse (binary tail risk, could spike VIX 2-3 points either way)
S&P 500 index addition decision on SPCX (if admitted, forces passive rebalancing into mega-cap blend, temporary bullish NQ cross-flow)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal SIGNS and oil supplies normalize (Strait of Hormuz flows fully reopen), risk-on sentiment could surge and cap real yields lower → NQ duration play extends higher. Conversely, if deal collapses and geopolitical vol spikes, VXN >20 could trigger tech deleveraging. SpaceX IPO capital drain is a one-day event; flow normalization back to mega-caps could reverse the micro-cap enthusiasm quickly. Confidence capped at 5 due to conflicting signals and absence of clear macro catalyst (CPI/FOMC timing unknown).
screening.entry_guidance: Move already in progress (SpaceX +17-20%, semis rallied). For NQ longs: wait for pullback to 19,300 support before adding; for shorts: wait for resistance re-test at 19,700 before shorting into FOMO. Current chop unsuitable for fresh directional bets; trade micro-structure and options flow instead.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO on Nasdaq with $1.77T valuation and +25% first-day pop is the dominant narrative. Elon Musk now ~$1.05T net worth (combining SpaceX + Tesla stakes). Large liquidity event and retail/institutional capital rotation into space/growth names.

factors.name: Iran peace deal hopes
factors.summary: US-Iran diplomatic breakthrough reported; Pakistan confirmed deal reached. Oil prices fell to ~$84/bbl (from ~$85), reducing inflation risk and de-risking energy-dependent equities. Strait of Hormuz reopening would ease supply concerns.

factors.name: Risk-on sentiment, crypto strength
factors.summary: Bitcoin rallied to $64.3K on improved risk mood; crypto volatility subsiding as peace trade materializes. Standard Chartered calls crypto bottom at $59K. Higher risk appetite typically favors growth/tech-heavy Nasdaq.

factors.name: Real yields & Fed rate trajectory unclear
factors.summary: No major inflation data (CPI/PCE) released in last 24h. BoJ hike expected Tuesday; Warsh's first Fed meeting next week. Market pricing uncertain; no clear signal on real rate direction near-term. Tech multiple support contingent on yields holding.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings calendar
factors.summary: No imminent mega-cap earnings announcements in news window (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA reporting later). Hyperscaler capex guidance absent; no new AI/semiconductor demand catalysts in past 24h.

factors.name: Dollar weakness on peace deal
factors.summary: USD/JPY steady ~160.2 despite oil decline; USD/CAD pulled back from 1.4023 highs as commodity weakness offsets safe-haven bid. Mixed signals; no strong directional conviction for dollar-sensitive tech.

factors.name: Price action & momentum
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.30% in 4h, +0.55% in 24h — muted moves despite SpaceX/Iran headlines. Dow outperformed (Nasdaq slipped red intraday per FXStreet). Suggests market is digesting catalyst rather than running hard higher.

factors.name: Antitrust & regulatory headwinds
factors.summary: DOJ 'anti-weaponization' fund blocked indefinitely; judge requires Trump admin guarantee fund is dead. EPA considering reversal of California emissions rules (potential EV/tech regulatory overhang). Micro-negative for mega-cap sentiment.

factors.name: SpaceX proxy unwinding
factors.summary: Space-related proxy stocks tumbled as real SpaceX IPO arrived; options volume booming pre-IPO collapsed post-listing. Suggests spec positioning unwind and potential profit-taking in related equities; rotation away from Nasdaq proxies.

factors.name: Bias history divergence
factors.summary: Prior reads showed drift from BUY (7/10 → 6/10 → NEUTRAL 5/10) over 8 hours. Fresh NEUTRAL stance suggests momentum fading and analyst conviction eroding as move stalls. No new confirmation triggers hit.

screening.narrative: SpaceX's historic $1.77T IPO and +25% debut on Nasdaq, combined with Iran peace deal optimism and oil price pullback (~$84/bbl), created a risk-on backdrop supporting Nasdaq strength. Bitcoin rallied to $64.3K and crypto volatility eased, reinforcing growth/tech appetite. However, NAS100's actual price action is subdued (+0.30% in 4h, +0.55% in 24h), Dow outperformed, and prior analyst bias has drifted from BUY to NEUTRAL, suggesting the initial enthusiasm is cooling. Space-related proxy stocks sold off hard as capital rotated into the real IPO, signaling potential profit-taking in surrounding equities. Absent fresh mega-cap earnings, hyperscaler capex guidance, or clearer Fed signals, the market appears to be digesting rather than chasing.
catalysts_ahead: BoJ rate decision & Kuroda successor policy guidance (Tuesday)
Kevin Warsh's first Federal Reserve meeting as Chair (next week)
Bank of England decision & UK CPI/employment data (18-19 June)
SpaceX options begin trading (Tuesday)
Potential Iran-US deal signing (imminent, within days per Trump admin)
Mega-cap tech earnings (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL — dates TBA)
US inflation data (CPI/PCE) — due mid-late June
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal falls through or deteriorates, oil will spike and growth multiple compression resumes, likely triggering sharp Nasdaq selloff. Conversely, if SpaceX momentum sustains and mega-caps pre-announce strong AI capex, Nasdaq could rapidly re-rate higher. BoJ hike Tuesday could strengthen yen and weaken risk appetite if hawkish surprise occurs. Real yields remain the key headwind for long-duration tech; any surprise hot inflation print would invalidate the NEUTRAL bias toward SELL.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (early digestion, muted price action). Avoid chasing here; wait for pullback to 29,500 support or fresh catalyst (BoJ Tuesday, major earnings, inflation surprise). If Iran deal is confirmed within 48h and holds above $84/bbl oil, consider modest long positioning on a retest of 29,800 resistance.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO surged 25% on debut, market cap topped $2T, Musk became world's first trillionaire. Massive retail participation and euphoria, but likely a one-day mania event with proxy stocks already unwinding gains.

factors.name: US-Iran peace deal progress
factors.summary: Pakistan announced framework deal close; oil fell below $85 (3-month low); Hormuz strait reopening expected. Risk-off sentiment easing, but deal not yet signed (Trump says 'not 100% certain')—geopolitical tail-risk premium deflating.

factors.name: Tech sector earnings & guidance
factors.summary: No major mega-cap earnings announced in this window. Adobe CFO departure signals software sector weakness vs. semiconductor/hardware plays. Meta admits 'mistakes' on AI transformation—mixed narrative.

factors.name: Fed policy & rate expectations
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh takes helm as Fed Chair next week; BoJ rate hike (25bps) widely expected Tuesday. Macro calendar heavy ahead; no major US inflation data released in this 24h window—catalyst-dependent rather than data-driven.

factors.name: Real yields & 10Y Treasury
factors.summary: No 10Y yield data in news window. Iran deal narrative and oil deflation could ease inflation concerns modestly, but macro backdrop remains uncertain without fresh CPI/PCE surprises.

factors.name: Antitrust & regulation
factors.summary: Paramount-WBD merger won DOJ approval (~$110B deal). Reduced regulatory friction for large-cap consolidation, though no direct Nasdaq 100 mega-cap benefit yet.

factors.name: Dollar direction (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY down 0.27% to ~99.80 on week; traders most optimistic on USD since Feb 2025 on haven demand from geopolitics. Mixed signals—geopolitical premium should fade if Iran deal signed, potentially weakening USD.

factors.name: Crypto correlation & risk-on
factors.summary: Bitcoin hit $64.3K session high on SpaceX euphoria and Iran deal optimism; Standard Chartered calls crypto bottom at $59K. Risk sentiment improving, but crypto volatility remains (down from $65K earlier)—not a conviction driver.

factors.name: Price action & momentum
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.46% in 24h, -0.22% in 4h. Minimal intraday movement despite massive SpaceX event. Suggests tech sector digesting euphoria without meaningful follow-through. Move is in progress but muted.

factors.name: Bias history continuity
factors.summary: Prior reads: BUY 6/10 → NEUTRAL 5/10 → NEUTRAL 5/10. Consistent downgrade from BUY to NEUTRAL over 7h. No confirmation triggers across reads—suggests caution and lack of conviction.

screening.narrative: SpaceX IPO mania is real but containable: the mega-event triggered euphoria in retail/risk-on trades (BTC +3.5%, oil -2.5%, gold +0.2%), but Nasdaq 100 itself is barely moving—up 0.46% in 24h despite the largest IPO in years. This suggests mega-cap tech (which dominate NDX) are pricing in Iran deal relief (lower inflation, lower oil, softer real yields eventually), but the macro backdrop remains genuinely uncertain. Fed policy shift with Warsh's arrival, BoJ hike Tuesday, and critical inflation reports (CPI likely next week) are still ahead. The SpaceX mania is a distraction, not a driver of NDX fundamentals.
catalysts_ahead: BoJ rate decision Tuesday (25bps widely expected) → USD/JPY, carry trade implications
Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting next week → policy tone shift signals
Iran deal signing (likely early next week) → geopolitical re-pricing, oil/bond yields
CPI/Core CPI (likely next week) → Fed expectations, real yield reset
Retail sales, FOMC minutes, NAHB housing data
Major tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN dates TBD) → forward guidance on cloud capex & AI
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal fails or geopolitical tensions re-escalate, risk-off reversal would flush SpaceX/retail euphoria and likely hit growth techs. Conversely, if CPI surprises hot next week, Fed hawkishness would slam real yields and tank NDX multiples. Warsh's debut may also surprise with more dovish tilt than markets expect, triggering a rally—but his governance stance is unknown.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2; SpaceX mania is a one-off, not a tech sector catalyst. Neutral bias suggests waiting for pullback to 29400–29500 support or for clarity on Iran deal + BoJ hike (Tue) + CPI release (likely Wed/Thu) before taking directional exposure. No conviction entry warranted until macro calendar settles.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event
factors.summary: SpaceX raised $75B in largest IPO ever, surged 25%+ to $2.2T market cap on June 12. Historic risk-on sentiment and mega-cap inclusion tailwinds could drive NDX higher, though some traders are unwinding SpaceX proxy positions and cautioning 'manic impulsiveness' in retail.

factors.name: Iran-US peace deal breakthrough
factors.summary: Trump administration reports framework deal near signing (Strait of Hormuz reopening, $10-20B Iran funds unlock, nuclear material terms). Oil fell below $85/bbl, reducing inflation pressure and boosting risk appetite across equities and crypto (Bitcoin +$64K).

factors.name: Tech sector momentum & mega-cap earnings absent
factors.summary: No major AAPL/MSFT/NVDA/GOOGL earnings or forward guidance in news window. SpaceX debut consumed bandwidth; hyperscaler capex commentary and semiconductor demand clarity still pending. Lack of specific tech earnings data limits conviction.

factors.name: Paramount-WBD merger DOJ approval
factors.summary: DOJ cleared $110B Paramount-Skydance-Warner deal on June 12, removing regulatory overhang. Modest positive for media, but not a primary NDX driver unless it signals broader antitrust softening.

factors.name: Sector rotation: value outpacing growth YTD
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports value stocks beating growth by wide margin in 2026, with investors broadening earnings bets beyond tech. This suggests relative underperformance of Nasdaq 100 (heavily growth-skewed) vs broader market.

factors.name: Fed policy week ahead (BoJ, BoE, RBA, Warsh debut)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first Federal Reserve meeting as Chair scheduled next week; BoJ 25bps hike expected Tuesday. Uncertainty over US rate trajectory and potential tightening bias could pressure high-duration growth stocks (Nasdaq's largest holdings). Intervention concerns in JPY also flag risk-off potential.

factors.name: No inflation or real yield data in window
factors.summary: No CPI, PCE, or real yield surprises reported in last 24h. Long-duration growth tech sensitivity to real rates is acute but unshaken by fresh data; 10-year Treasuries not highlighted as movers.

factors.name: Bitcoin & crypto sentiment improving
factors.summary: Bitcoin rallied to $64K+ after $59K low, with Standard Chartered calling cycle bottom in. Crypto inflows and risk-on mood are historically correlated with NDX outperformance, reducing safe-haven demand.

factors.name: Price action: flat 4h, modest 24h gain
factors.summary: NAS100 down 0.22% in 4h, up only 0.46% in 24h. No sustained directional move; still consolidating despite SpaceX IPO boost and Iran deal optimism. Suggests market waiting for confirmation (earnings, Fed guidance, macro data).

factors.name: Insider selling & CEO stock sales
factors.summary: Multiple Form 4 filings reported: HeartFlow CEO ($643K), Cryoport CTO ($507K), Dell director ($16M) sell-offs. While normal, concentrated selling into market strength can signal caution among insiders.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 remains in consolidation despite Friday's historic SpaceX IPO debut (+25%) and breakthrough on Iran-US peace deal (oil down, risk appetite up). While SpaceX mega-IPO and geopolitical de-escalation are strongly bullish for risk assets, the value-over-growth rotation and upcoming heavy central bank calendar (BoJ hike Tuesday, Warsh debut) are tempering conviction. Tech earnings season is absent from the news flow, leaving growth multiples vulnerable to any hawkish Fed surprise or real yield surprise. The index is flat intraday and modest up on the day—classic Phase 1-to-2 transition where initial optimism lacks follow-through confirmation.
catalysts_ahead: Bank of Japan (BoJ) 25bps rate hike decision — Tuesday, 2026-06-17 (~likely, per newsflow)
Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting — mid-week, 2026-06-17–18
Bank of England (BoE) policy decision — week of June 16–19
US Retail Sales data (June) — likely week of June 16–20
Iran-US peace deal formal signing — reportedly imminent, possibly Monday-Tuesday
NVIDIA, Microsoft, Apple, Google earnings (if any scheduled for week of June 16)
CPI / Core CPI release (if scheduled)
screening.risk_warning: Hawkish BoJ hike or Warsh hawkish pivot could trigger real yield spike, compressing NDX valuations. Failure of Iran deal to close or escalation of Middle East tensions could reverse risk-on trade and trigger flight-to-safety (sell tech, buy Treasuries). Absence of earnings confirmation means SpaceX IPO euphoria could fade quickly if mega-cap tech reports disappoint or cuts guidance. Value outpacing growth YTD suggests structural headwinds for growth-heavy Nasdaq 100.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is partially underway (SpaceX/Iran euphoria priced in 0.46% gain). Wait for retest of support at ~29,400 or clear break above 30,000 resistance on BoJ/Warsh clarity before committing. Do not chase here; conviction rises only if Fed signals dovish surprise or earnings confirm hyperscaler strength.
